item.text: {"outcome":"fail","confidence":"Certain","findings":[{"check":"A-SEC-6","title":"Reset tokens are generated with predictable, non-cryptographic randomness","severity":"High","path":"src/reset.js","line":6,"quote":"const token = `${Date.now().toString(36)}${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;"},{"check":"A-SEC-6","title":"Reset-token verification does not consume the token, so it is reusable until expiry","severity":"High","path":"src/reset.js","line":16,"quote":"if (!record || record.used || record.expiresAt < Date.now()) return false;"}],"reasoning":"bcrypt cost 12 is used for password hashing and bcrypt.compare for verification. Reset-token comparison uses timingSafeEqual, and the password reset limiter is mounted before its route. However, reset tokens use Date.now and Math.random instead of a CSPRNG, and verification checks record.used without ever marking the record used."}
